Title: Molecular regulation of the salicylic acid hormone pathway in plants under changing environmental conditions

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity and stress responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker of SA signaling; expression increases with cold or beneficial microbes and decreases with high humidity or warm temperature
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PR1 is a canonical SA-responsive marker gene downstream of NPR1/TGA signaling. 2. Its expression rises under low temperature and certain beneficial microbe interactions. 3. It is strongly reduced by high humidity and warm temperatures, mirroring SA suppression.
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Cucumis sativus; Phaseolus vulgaris
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Cucumis sativus; Phaseolus vulgaris
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by low temperature, low humidity, beneficial microbes; repressed by high humidity and high temperature
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold; high humidity; low humidity; warm temperature; rhizobacterial inoculation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Defense activation may incur growth costs
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful reporter/marker for selecting SA pathway resilience rather than direct engineering target
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a commonly summarized SA-response output gene, not a biosynthetic/regulatory master gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity and stress responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker of SA signaling that is decreased by high humidity and high temperature
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PR2 is an SA-responsive defense marker gene. 2. Basal PR2 transcripts are considerably decreased at high relative humidity. 3. PR2 is also downregulated at high temperature, consistent with reduced SA signaling.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced under high humidity and high temperature
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Defense activation may incur growth costs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker/output gene rather than core biosynthetic determinant.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity and stress responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediate NPR1-dependent activation or NPR3/4-dependent repression of SA-responsive genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGA transcription factors bind promoters of SA-responsive genes such as PR genes. 2. NPR1 acts with TGAs to activate defense transcription. 3. NPR3/NPR4 interact with TGAs to repress transcription in the absence of SA.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SA status; drought context through NPR3/4; cold context through NPR1-independent branch
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Promoters of PR genes and other SA-responsive genes
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering broad defense transcription could affect growth-defense balance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential signaling leverage point, but broad pleiotropy expected
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as downstream transcription partners rather than primary focus of the review.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity and stress responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Act upstream of CBP60g/SARD1 to activate SA biosynthesis in PTI
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PCRK1 and PCRK2 function between PAMP receptors and CBP60g/SARD1. 2. They help connect early immune perception to activation of SA biosynthesis. 3. Their role is highlighted as part of PTI inputs into environmentally sensitive SA regulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized prior kinase genetics and PTI studies
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may broadly alter immune signaling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible upstream node to strengthen inducible SA production
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive upstream signaling components, not emphasized as primary review focus.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity and stress responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially links heat sensing/calcium signaling to SA suppression at high temperature
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CNGCs generate immune-related calcium signals and also function as plasma membrane thermosensors. 2. The review proposes that CNGCs may contribute to SA suppression at heat stress temperatures. 3. A mechanistic connection to SA biosynthesis remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized prior calcium-channel and thermosensing studies; no direct SA mechanism established in this review
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad roles in thermotolerance and immunity may cause pleiotropy
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for heat-resilient immunity, but direct SA control is not yet proven
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review treats CNGCs as a hypothetical/putative hot-temperature node for SA regulation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity and stress responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PTI marker gene downregulated by high temperature and elevated CO2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FRK1 is a prominent PTI marker gene. 2. It is downregulated under high temperature and elevated CO2. 3. Whether CBP60g/SARD1 directly control FRK1 under these environments remains unknown.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced under high temperature and high CO2
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic data summarized from cited studies
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful biomarker for environment-sensitive immune competence
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker of upstream PTI rather than SA pathway enzyme/regulator.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity and stress responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper distal SA transport; transport defects can be rescued by high humidity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. acp4 cuticle mutant shows perturbed distal SA transport. 2. This defect can be rescued by high humidity. 3. The finding links water transpiration/cuticle properties to systemic SA movement.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered cuticle may affect water loss and pathogen interactions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates transport-related environmental sensitivity, but likely not a direct metabolic engineering target
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport-associated common gene, not biosynthetic/signaling master regulator.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity and stress responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cuticle mutant with altered distal SA transport rescued by high humidity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mod1 mutant perturbs distal SA transport. 2. High humidity rescues the transport defect. 3. This supports a role for cuticle/transpiration in systemic SA movement.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Cuticle/FA defects likely have strong pleiotropy
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Mechanistic insight for SA transport biology rather than practical target

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation under chilling and aluminum stress conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL is a key enzyme in one SA biosynthetic route from phenylalanine. 2. Chilling upregulates PAL and/or its activity in cucumber, Astragalus membranaceus, and Arabidopsis, increasing SA accumulation. 3. Aluminum stress in soybean roots increases PAL activity together with SA and stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized prior expression/activity analyses and SA measurements under chilling or aluminum stress
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Cucumis sativus; Astragalus membranaceus; Glycine max
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Cucumis sativus; Astragalus membranaceus; Glycine max
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after chilling; activity increased under aluminum stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots then leaves in cucumber after chilling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance stress-associated SA accumulation, but pathway redundancy with ICS branch should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes PAL as one of two SA biosynthetic routes; downstream conversion from cinnamate to SA remains less fully defined than ICS route.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Presumably converts benzoic acid to salicylic acid by 2-hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased SA accumulation under chilling and aluminum stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BA2H is proposed to convert benzoic acid into SA in the PAL pathway. 2. Chilling upregulates BA2H in cucumber and Astragalus membranaceus. 3. Aluminum stress in soybean roots increases BA2H activity together with SA accumulation and metal tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized prior expression/activity analyses and SA measurements
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Cucumis sativus; Astragalus membranaceus; Glycine max; Nicotiana tabacum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Cucumis sativus; Astragalus membranaceus; Glycine max
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated/increased activity after chilling or aluminum stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots then leaves in cucumber after chilling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for SA enhancement in stress tolerance, though assignment remains partly presumptive in plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review explicitly notes BA2H presumably converts benzoic acid into SA.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/lignin pathway linked to SA-related stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream of PAL, upstream of lignin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts cinnamic acid to coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Indirect/associated with SA-related stress adaptation rather than direct SA formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased C4H expression correlates with elevated SA levels and salinity resilience in safflower
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H converts cinnamic acid to coumaric acid for lignin production. 2. Under high salt stress, C4H expression increases in safflower together with SA levels. 3. This increase is associated with enhanced abiotic stress resilience.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized expression analysis under salinity stress
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be considered in stress-resilience breeding, but direct causal role in SA biosynthesis remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes it remains unknown whether salinity-induced SA production is controlled through mechanisms similar to chilling.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate into isochorismate in the chloroplast
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA biosynthesis; expression is increased by cold, low humidity, and salt stress but suppressed by warm temperature and elevated CO2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS1 catalyzes the chloroplast step from chorismate to isochorismate in the major SA biosynthetic pathway. 2. Its expression shows condition-dependent induction by prolonged cold, low humidity, and salt stress. 3. Basal and pathogen-induced ICS1 expression and SA biosynthesis are suppressed at warm temperatures and by elevated CO2, increasing pathogen susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized mutant genetics, expression analyses, pathogen assays, and SA metabolite measurements from cited studies
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Cucumis sativus; Carthamus tinctorius
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Cucumis sativus; Carthamus tinctorius
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by prolonged cold, low humidity, and salinity; reduced by warm temperature and elevated CO2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold; warm temperature; elevated CO2; low humidity; salt stress; pathogen infection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; roots in some chilling studies
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point; major SA biosynthetic enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major engineering target to boost SA-mediated immunity, but thermosensitive suppression under warming must be addressed together with upstream regulators
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ICS2 is noted as functionally redundant and may be temporally induced after short-term chilling.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: May compensate for ICS1 under short-term chilling in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS2 is described as functionally redundant with ICS1. 2. It is upregulated after chilling when ICS1 is downregulated in one Arabidopsis study. 3. This suggests temporal regulation of the two ICS orthologs under cold stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized transcript analysis from prior cold-stress study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible backup target for stabilizing SA synthesis under fluctuating cold regimes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as redundant and temporally regulated rather than as a central focus.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Exports isochorismate from the chloroplast to the cytoplasm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for SA biosynthesis; expression is reduced at warm temperatures and induced with low humidity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EDS5 exports isochorismate from chloroplast to cytoplasm in the ICS pathway. 2. EDS5 is directly controlled by CBP60g and SARD1. 3. Its transcript abundance is downregulated under warmer temperatures but upregulated under low humidity together with enhanced SA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized prior genetic studies and transcript analyses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at lower humidity; downregulated at warm temperatures
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Warm temperature; low humidity; pathogen infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to maintain SA precursor export under warming, likely best combined with upstream regulatory modules
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as pathway gene here because it functions as an essential dedicated step within the ICS SA pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts exported isochorismate into isochorismate-9-glutamate en route to salicylic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for SA production in the ICS pathway; expression is downregulated at warm temperatures
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBS3 acts downstream of EDS5 in the cytosolic ICS pathway to drive SA formation. 2. It is directly regulated by CBP60g and SARD1. 3. Transcriptomic data indicate that PBS3 is downregulated at warmer temperatures, contributing to reduced SA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized biochemical pathway reconstitution, genetics, and transcriptomics from cited studies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to sustain SA biosynthesis under elevated temperature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper summarizes PBS3 as one of the core direct targets of CBP60g/SARD1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts downstream of PBS3 to complete conversion of isochorismate-derived intermediate to salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes SA biosynthesis in the ICS pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EPS1 functions downstream of PBS3 in the Arabidopsis ICS pathway. 2. It completes isochorismate-derived SA biosynthesis. 3. The review presents EPS1 as a pathway component rather than an environmentally characterized node.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized cited biochemical and genetic studies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be stacked with ICS1/PBS3 to reconstruct efficient SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is summarized as a core enzyme completing SA biosynthesis in review Figure 2.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts inactive methyl salicylate to active salicylic acid in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely increases active SA during chilling-associated cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MES9 hydrolyzes inactive MeSA to active SA in vitro. 2. MES9 transcripts increase after 10 h chilling at 4°C. 3. This is proposed to contribute to greater active SA and increased cold tolerance, although in vivo function is unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized transcriptomics and prior in vitro enzyme evidence
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful to remobilize active SA during cold adaptation, but requires in vivo validation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review explicitly notes that the in vivo function of MES9 is unknown.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts salicylic acid to methyl salicylate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product pool by converting SA into derivative
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Warm temperature upregulates pathogen-induced BSMT1 expression, potentially reducing active SA by promoting MeSA formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BSMT1 encodes the enzyme that methylates SA to MeSA. 2. Warm temperature upregulates pathogen-induced BSMT1 expression. 3. This indicates that SA catabolic/conversion routes are environmentally regulated and may contribute to reduced active SA under warming.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized transcriptome data and prior biochemical gene assignment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen-induced expression upregulated by warm temperature
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulation/editing of BSMT1 could help retain active SA under warm conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Relevant to SA homeostasis rather than de novo biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SA biosynthetic and accumulation genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator of SA production, partially redundant with SARD1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation and immunity; downregulation under warm temperature and drought suppresses SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBP60g directly regulates ICS pathway genes and other SA-associated genes. 2. It is a major positive regulator of SA biosynthesis in PTI and under environmentally permissive conditions. 3. Warm temperature and drought suppress CBP60g expression, reducing SA-mediated immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized mutant analysis, ChIP-seq, transcriptional profiling, pathogen assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by PTI/calcium signaling; reduced under warm temperature and drought
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: PAMP-triggered immunity; intracellular calcium influx; PCRK1/PCRK2 signaling; temperature; drought
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Warm temperature; drought; pathogen infection; PTI
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Associated with GDAC-dependent transcriptional activation module at its locus
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of growth-defense trade-off through SA elevation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in reviewed Arabidopsis studies
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq (reported in cited study Sun et al. 2015)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target for engineering temperature-resilient SA immunity, ideally combined with safeguarding of upstream thermosensitive modules
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes CBP60g/SARD1 as central environmentally sensitive node; paper figure states they are rate-limiting for maintaining SA-mediated immunity at elevated temperatures
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Warm temperatures reduce GBPL3 condensate-mediated recruitment of transcriptional machinery at CBP60g locus

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant-specific transcription factor; CBP60 family-related immune regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SA biosynthetic and accumulation genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator of SA production, partially redundant with CBP60g
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation and immunity; suppressed by warm temperature and drought
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD1 directly controls multiple genes required for SA biosynthesis and accumulation. 2. It acts redundantly with CBP60g as a master positive regulator of SA production. 3. Warm temperature and drought downregulate SARD1, compromising SA-mediated defense.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized mutant analysis, ChIP-seq, transcriptional profiling, pathogen assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by PTI/calcium signaling; reduced under warm temperature and drought
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: PAMP-triggered immunity; intracellular calcium influx; PCRK1/PCRK2 signaling; temperature; drought
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Warm temperature; drought; pathogen infection; PTI
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Associated with GDAC-dependent transcriptional activation module at its locus
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of growth-defense trade-off through SA elevation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in reviewed Arabidopsis studies
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq (reported in cited study Sun et al. 2015)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for strengthening climate-resilient immunity by stabilizing SA biosynthesis under warming and drought
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review highlights CBP60g/SARD1 as a rate-limiting node to maintain SA-mediated immunity at elevated temperatures
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Warm temperatures reduce GBPL3 condensate-mediated recruitment of transcriptional machinery at SARD1 locus

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Calmodulin-binding transcription activator; calmodulin-binding domain; IQ motif; transcriptional activation domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses CBP60g and SARD1, thereby suppressing ICS1-mediated SA production
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major thermosensitive repressor of SA pathway at ambient temperatures
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses SA biosynthesis at ambient temperatures; repression is relieved at low temperatures
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAMTA3 represses CBP60g/SARD1 and SA pathway genes at ambient temperatures. 2. Low temperature abrogates CAMTA-mediated suppression, enabling SA accumulation and cold-associated immunity. 3. Calmodulin binding and specific residues in the IQ domain and phosphoserines are important for SA suppression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized mutant analyses, domain-function studies, gene expression assays under low temperature and infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions at ambient temperatures; cold relieves suppression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts as calmodulin-regulated transcriptional repressor module
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong SA repression may favor growth at ambient temperature but reduce immunity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing thermosensitive regulatory features of CAMTA3 could help create temperature-resilient SA immunity while preserving growth
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review also refers to redundant CAMTA family members more broadly; CAMTA3 is the most mechanistically detailed member

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulate SARD1 and CBP60g expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant repressors that set basal SA level and cold responsiveness
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulate SA biosynthesis and N-hydroxypipecolic acid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAMTA transcription factors redundantly repress SA biosynthesis by modulating SARD1 and CBP60g. 2. Their repression is especially relevant across temperature responses. 3. They also link cold/freezing tolerance with immune pathway control.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized genetics and transcript profiling from cited studies
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Balancing growth/freezing tolerance and immune output
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Family-level tuning may provide a route to recalibrate basal SA without overactivating defense
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review repeatedly emphasizes CAMTAs as a major thermosensitive node, beyond CAMTA3 alone

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely transcriptional regulation; exact mechanism unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Thermoresponsive regulator of basal SA levels at nonstress temperatures
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Modulates thermosensitive SA levels and immunity between 16°C and 22°C
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural variation identified bHLH059 as a thermoresponsive regulator of SA-mediated immunity in Arabidopsis. 2. Two SNPs distinguish temperature-sensitive versus temperature-resilient accessions. 3. Increased bHLH059 expression at 22°C correlates inversely with SA levels in temperature-sensitive accessions.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural variation mapping/GWAS-style analysis, accession comparison, transcript analysis, SA phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher at 22°C than 16°C in temperature-sensitive accessions; unchanged in resilient accessions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May contribute to balancing thermoresponsive growth-defense outputs
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Natural variation/genotyping and transcriptomics summarized from cited study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Allele mining or editing of thermosensitive bHLH059 variants could stabilize SA immunity under mild warming
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes mechanism is unresolved and likely independent of phyB/PIF4 thermomorphogenesis module

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SA signaling; crosstalk with JA pathway; cold-responsive transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional coactivation; protein interaction-mediated repression of JA module
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PR genes; MYC2-MED25 JA transcriptional module; cold-induced HSFA1-associated genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SA-responsive genes with TGAs; represses JA outputs by suppressing MYC2-MED25 interaction; activates cold-induced genes with HSFA1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central SA receptor/coactivator controlling downstream response competence
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA signaling, cold tolerance, and disease resistance; targeted by humidity and drought; can amplify immunity with GA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPR1 is a central SA receptor/coactivator whose expression, stability, monomerization, and nuclear localization are environmentally regulated. 2. Cold increases NPR1 protein accumulation, monomerization, and nuclear localization, promoting cold tolerance and disease resistance via HSFA1 factors. 3. Drought and high humidity dampen NPR1-mediated SA signaling, while NPR1 also antagonizes JA signaling by disrupting MYC2-MED25 interactions.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized genetics, protein interaction assays, nuclear localization, ubiquitination/degradation studies, transcript analyses
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated/accumulated under cold and aluminum stress; downregulated by drought; post-transcriptionally regulated by high humidity and elevated CO2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: SA; cold; drought; humidity; elevated CO2; GA; cytokinin
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold; drought; high humidity; elevated CO2; aluminum stress; GA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TGA factors; NPR3; NPR4; HSFA1a; HSFA1b; HSFA1d; HSFA1e; MYC2; MED25
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in transcriptional activation complexes with TGAs and in antagonistic complex logic with MYC2/MED25
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of SA receptor homeostatic network with NPR3/NPR4-mediated turnover
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated SA/NPR1 activity contributes to growth-defense trade-offs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority target for climate-resilient immunity; strategies should preserve beneficial cold-responsive and anti-JA functions while preventing humidity/warmth-associated attenuation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review indicates NPR1 locus recruitment by GBPL3 is temperature-insensitive, suggesting NPR1 itself is relatively temperature resilient compared with CBP60g/SARD1 node
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: High humidity promotes NPR1 ubiquitination and degradation; elevated CO2 affects NPR1 post-transcriptionally

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: SA receptor paralog and transcriptional corepressor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional corepression; promotes proteasomal degradation of NPR1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TGA-associated SA-responsive genes; NPR1 protein stability
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses SA-responsive transcription; negatively affects CBP60g/SARD1 under drought context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative SA receptor that dampens SA responses under stress
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated in drought-associated repression of SA signaling and biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPR3 acts oppositely to NPR1 as a transcriptional corepressor in SA signaling. 2. It is thought to bind SA and control proteasomal degradation of NPR1. 3. NPR3 contributes with NPR4 to drought-mediated negative regulation of CBP60g/SARD1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized receptor genetics and transcriptional studies from cited work
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of SA homeostatic receptor network controlling NPR1 turnover
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess activity may weaken immunity under water deficit
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing stress-induced NPR3 repression could help preserve SA defenses during drought, but may incur growth or water-balance costs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review groups NPR3/4 together for drought-mediated repression

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: SA receptor paralog and transcriptional corepressor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional corepression; promotes proteasomal degradation of NPR1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TGA-associated SA-responsive genes; NPR1 protein stability
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses SA-responsive transcription; negatively affects CBP60g/SARD1 under drought context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative SA receptor that dampens SA responses under stress
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated in drought-associated repression of SA signaling and biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPR4 is an NPR1 paralog functioning as a transcriptional corepressor. 2. It is thought to bind SA and regulate NPR1 degradation. 3. NPR4 contributes with NPR3 to drought-mediated suppression of CBP60g/SARD1 and SA defenses.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized receptor genetics and transcriptional studies from cited work
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of SA homeostatic receptor network controlling NPR1 turnover
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess activity may weaken immunity under water deficit
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential editing target to sustain SA signaling during drought, with caution about runaway defense activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review groups NPR3/4 together for drought-mediated repression

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Temperature-sensitive transcriptional activation of SA biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Biomolecular condensate-mediated recruitment of transcription machinery
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes transcription of CBP60g and SARD1 via GDAC formation and RNA polymerase II/Mediator recruitment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller required to maintain SA-mediated immunity at elevated temperatures
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-induced SA biosynthesis; warm temperature reduces GDACs and thereby suppresses CBP60g/SARD1 and SA immunity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GBPL3 forms defense-activated condensates that drive immune transcriptional responses. 2. Elevated temperature selectively reduces GDAC formation at CBP60g/SARD1 loci. 3. Loss of GDACs abolishes recruitment of RNA polymerase II and specific Mediator subunits, reducing SA biosynthesis and immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized phase-separation analyses, locus recruitment assays, transcriptional studies, temperature comparison
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Recruited to target loci upon immune activation; temperature-sensitive at CBP60g/SARD1 but not NPR1 locus
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection; immune elicitation; warm temperature
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GBPL1; RNA polymerase II; Mediator subunits
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms GDAC biomolecular condensates; also component of nuclear pore complex
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Temperature-sensitive loss of function may compromise immunity under warming
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering thermoresilient condensate formation or target-locus recruitment could sustain SA immunity under warming
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review proposes GBPL3/GDACs as potential thermosensor-like node; NPR1 locus recruitment remains temperature-insensitive

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GDAC assembly affecting SA biosynthetic transcription indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly influences CBP60g/SARD1 activation by controlling GBPL3 sequestration
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports immune condensate switch that impacts SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Under basal conditions sequesters GBPL3; displacement upon immune activation enables GDAC formation and SA-promoting transcription
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GBPL1 and GBPL3 are the two key proteins forming GDACs. 2. GBPL1 sequesters GBPL3 under basal conditions. 3. Immune activation displaces this interaction to permit GBPL3 phase separation and downstream activation of SA-related transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized phase-separation and protein interaction analyses from cited work
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Basal sequestration role; altered upon immune activation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential accessory engineering target for stabilizing thermoresilient GDAC behavior
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because review identifies GBPL1 as one of two key proteins in GDAC formation, although emphasis is mainly on GBPL3

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA pathway antagonism toward SA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of JA genes; antagonized by NPR1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates JA-responsive genes; its interaction with MED25 is suppressed by NPR1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major antagonistic regulator that counterbalances SA-mediated immunity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant immunity, growth, development, and abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Through JA signaling, antagonizes SA-mediated immunity; NPR1 suppresses MYC2-MED25 interactions to fine-tune defense balance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2 is a key JA transcription activator antagonistic to SA-mediated immunity. 2. NPR1 physically suppresses MYC2 interaction with MED25 on JA target promoters. 3. This interaction framework fine-tunes balance between JA and SA pathways, especially relevant to microbiome-induced JA activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized protein interaction and transcriptional studies from cited work
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated in JA pathway context; modulated by rhizobacterial signals/VOCs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Works with MED25 at JA target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of hormone crosstalk network with antagonistic SA-JA regulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shifts defense toward JA can weaken SA-mediated resistance to biotrophs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful leverage point to rebalance microbiome-responsive JA-SA crosstalk, but broad developmental roles necessitate caution
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a central SA biosynthetic regulator but a decisive crosstalk node highlighted in the review
